description: Fix two verified bugs found during backend code audit: data loss in HAR export clear path, and hanging SSE client in dev proxy on upstream rejection.

userStories.title: Fix data loss in export-har when WebSocket frames clear fetch fails
userStories.description: As a developer using browser_export_har with clear=true and includeWebSocketFrames=true, I want the tool to not lose data if the WebSocket frames clearing fetch fails, so that I can always recover my captured traffic.
acceptanceCriteria: When clear=true and includeWebSocketFrames=true, the HTTP buffer is NOT cleared until ALL clear fetches (HTTP + WS) succeed
If the WS frames clear fetch fails, the error propagates and no data has been lost from either the entries array or the extension buffers
The existing non-clearing initial fetches (lines 204-214) remain unchanged — they serve as the baseline data
Only after both clearing fetches succeed are the entries replaced with the cleared data
Existing unit tests in export-har.test.ts continue to pass
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/export-har.ts, lines 221-248.

Root cause: When args.clear is true, the code first fetches HTTP requests with clear:true (line 222-230), then sets entries.length=0 (line 232) and repopulates with the cleared HTTP data. Then if includeWebSocketFrames is true, it fetches WS frames with clear:true (lines 236-244). If THIS second clear fetch fails (dispatch timeout, extension crash, validation error), the HTTP buffer in the extension has already been cleared by the first fetch, and the original entries (HTTP + WS from the non-clearing fetches at lines 204-214) have been wiped by entries.length=0. The user cannot recover the original HTTP data.

Correct fix: Do NOT clear entries or mutate the extension buffers until ALL clearing fetches succeed. Collect all clearing results into local variables first, then swap entries only after everything succeeds. Specifically:
1. Fetch clearRequestsRaw with clear:true
2. Validate clearRequests
3. If includeWebSocketFrames, fetch clearFramesRaw with clear:true
4. Validate clearFrames
5. Only THEN set entries.length=0 and repopulate

WARNING: The HTTP clear:true fetch both returns data AND clears the buffer atomically (it's a single dispatch to the extension). So you cannot avoid clearing the HTTP buffer — the issue is that we should NOT wipe the entries array until the WS clear also succeeds. If the WS clear fails, we still have the HTTP data from clearRequests and can use it. The fix should:
- Collect clearRequests into a local variable
- Collect clearFrames into a local variable (if needed)
- Only after both succeed, rebuild entries from the cleared data
- If WS clear fails, let the error propagate — clearRequests data is lost but this is acceptable since the HTTP clearing fetch is atomic and cannot be un-done. The key improvement is NOT wiping entries before we know all fetches will succeed, so the error path doesn't discard the initial non-clearing fetch results.

userStories.title: Fix dev proxy SSE client hanging on upstream non-200 response
userStories.description: As a developer using dev mode, I want the dev proxy to handle upstream SSE rejection gracefully, so that MCP clients don't hang indefinitely when the worker rejects a GET /mcp request.
acceptanceCriteria: When connectUpstreamSse receives a non-200 status code from upstream, the session is cleaned up and the client response is closed
The proxy logs a warning when upstream SSE connection is rejected with a non-200 status
Existing dev-proxy.test.ts tests continue to pass
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/dev-proxy.ts, lines 312-316.

Root cause: In connectUpstreamSse(), when the upstream worker responds with a non-200 status (e.g., 400 because the session ID is stale after worker restart), the code calls upstreamRes.resume() to drain the response, then returns. But clientRes (the MCP client's GET /mcp SSE stream) is never closed or sent an error. The client hangs forever waiting for SSE events.

This is different from the intentional 'keep open for reconnection' pattern used in the on('end') and on('error') handlers (lines 328-335). Those cases handle a previously-successful SSE stream that closes due to worker restart — keeping the client open is correct there because reinitializeSessions() will reconnect. But a non-200 response means the upstream explicitly rejected the request, and no reconnection will happen until the next worker restart.

Correct fix: When upstreamRes.statusCode !== 200, call cleanupSession() for this session's proxySessionId (imported from the module scope), then end the clientRes with an appropriate HTTP error status. If headers haven't been sent yet on clientRes, send a 502 Bad Gateway. If headers were already sent (SSE stream was partially open), just destroy the clientRes.

Alternatively, a simpler fix: remove the session's SSE stream entry so reinitializeSessions() doesn't try to reconnect it, and end clientRes. The MCP client will detect the disconnect and create a new session.

WARNING: Do NOT change the on('end') or on('error') handlers — those intentionally keep the client response open for reconnection after worker restart. Only change the non-200 status code path.
